sector.name: Co-Working
sector.description: Flex workspace operators targeting enterprise & GCC clients across India

fyPerformance: FY26 Revenue: ₹1,493 Cr (+24% YoY); PAT: ₹71 Cr (+66% YoY)
Added 41 centers & 30,000 seats in FY26 — total 266 centers across 18 cities
Operating EBITDA: ₹550 Cr (+37%); EBITDA margin 36.8% — highest ever
highlights: Deliberate shift to Grade A/A+ buildings only — quality over quantity in seat additions
Partial Managed Office format (40–60% anchor pre-commitment) gaining traction as new model
GCC and domestic enterprise demand driving occupancy recovery toward 84% target
New CFO Sumit Rochlani appointed Feb 2026; deep Awfis institutional continuity assured
AWFIS Space Solutions Ltd.quote: "As we enter FY27, the business is in its strongest position yet. Healthy occupancy, expanding margins, industry-leading returns on capital, and a quality pipeline already committed. We enter FY27 with clarity of strategy, depth of execution, and full confidence in what lies ahead."

fyPerformance: FY26 Revenue: ₹1,037 Cr (+58% YoY) — crossed ₹1,000 Cr milestone
FY26 PAT: ₹235 Cr (+67% YoY); net margin expanded to 22.6% (+120 bps)
117 centers, 78,782 seats, 25 cities; occupancy sustained above 90%
highlights: ~70% of leasing revenue from large enterprise clients — long lock-in, stable anchor base
Furniture vertical surged +177% YoY in Q4FY26 — emerging as a high-margin contributor
ROCE: 33%; asset-backed borrowings at 7.5–7.75% — tight balance sheet discipline
MD on AI: 'AI will generate employment, not reduce it' — structurally bullish on office demand
EFC (I) Ltd.quote: "Our Q4 FY26 performance reflects steady execution and the growing acceptance of our integrated workspace ecosystem. With strong and consistent growth across the board, we remain confident about sustaining growth momentum. The leasing business continues to remain strong."

fyPerformance: FY26 Net Loss: ₹106 Cr (narrowed 24% from ₹140 Cr in FY25)
17 cities, 9.55 mn sq ft managed; GCCs constitute >40% of portfolio
Indiqube Spaces Ltd.outlook: Management highlighted sustained GCC demand as the primary growth engine for FY27. Three major GCC deals were closed in Q4FY26 — ₹52 Cr (Japanese e-commerce GCC, Bengaluru, 5-year), ₹75 Cr (healthcare-tech GCC), and ₹54 Cr (GCC client, Pune) — demonstrating strong enterprise pipeline visibility. Management framed India's flex market crossing 100 mn sq ft as a structural inflection point, positioning Indiqube as the preferred managed workspace partner for international GCCs entering India. No formal numeric FY27 guidance was provided; the focus is on driving occupancy to profitability thresholds rather than aggressive seat expansion. Analyst consensus pegs FY27 revenue growth at ~27%.
highlights: GCCs >40% of portfolio; positioned as the preferred GCC-entry partner for global enterprises
Q4 deal wins: ₹52 Cr + ₹75 Cr + ₹54 Cr across 3 major GCC clients — deal momentum intact
Promoters bought 1.14 lakh shares voluntarily in Q4FY26 — a clear insider confidence signal
Path to profitability in FY27 is the key catalyst to watch; losses narrowing sharply YoY
Indiqube Spaces Ltd.quote: "As India's flex office market crosses 100 million sq ft, Indiqube is positioned as the preferred partner for international companies establishing GCC operations in India."

fyPerformance: FY26 Revenue: ₹1,796 Cr (+31% YoY); Normalised PAT: ₹97 Cr (+453% YoY)
First listed flex platform to cross 10 msf operational; 16.1 msf total (incl. LOIs)
ROCE: 16% (doubled from 7.3% in FY25); Net debt: −₹56 Cr (first net-cash positive)
Smartworks Coworking Spaces Ltd.outlook: Management is confident in a compounding flywheel — occupancy, premiumisation, and operating leverage reinforcing cash generation and capital returns quarter after quarter. No explicit FY27 revenue guidance was issued, but 100% supply visibility for FY27 and 75% for FY28 signal strong structural confidence. The focus remains on deepening enterprise relationships in the 1,000+ seat cohort (now 37% of revenue, avg. tenure >48 months), and converting the fixed-lease cost structure into operating leverage as occupancy scales from 82% toward maturity. GCC revenue, crossing 15% of rental revenue, is the key diversification from IT/ITeS concentration. SmartVantage product expected to materially contribute to FY27 bottom line.
highlights: 100% FY27 supply visibility; 75% FY28 already secured — strong committed pipeline
Gross debt declined >50% since IPO (Jul 2025); borrowing cost fell 180 bps to below 9%
1,000+ seat cohort: 29% → 37% of revenue; avg. tenure >48 months — powerful retention moat
GCC revenue crossing 15% — key diversification away from IT/ITeS client concentration
Occupancy at 82% (dipped 70 bps on 0.9 msf new capacity in Q4); ramp-up ongoing in FY27
Smartworks Coworking Spaces Ltd.quote: "We are evolving beyond traditional coworking into a corporate infrastructure provider. Occupancy, premiumisation, and operating leverage continue to reinforce each other quarter after quarter."

fyPerformance: Net debt: −₹12 Cr (first-ever net-cash positive); credit rating upgraded A− → A+
76 centers, 8.6 msf operational; 11.6 msf committed (+39% YoY); 86.9% occupancy
Wework India Management Ltd.outlook: Management tone was emphatically bullish. Karan Virwani positioned WeWork India as 'no longer just a workspace operator' — pivoting to an enterprise infrastructure platform narrative. No explicit FY27 revenue guidance was provided. The compounding flywheel thesis was central: occupancy → premiumisation → operating leverage → cash generation → reinvestment. The 11.6 msf committed footprint (+39% YoY) and 700,000+ sq ft of new expansion underway in Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Chennai indicate sustained double-digit revenue growth ahead. Mature center occupancy at 88.9% signals near-saturation — strong premium pricing power in core assets. Key deal: ₹475 Cr managed office contract with TMUS India signed in Q4.
highlights: First-ever net-cash positive balance sheet (−₹12 Cr net debt) — structural deleveraging complete
Credit upgrade A− → A+; cost of borrowing fell 225 bps to 8.5% — balance sheet strength
Q4FY26 ROCE: 45.1% — best-in-class capital efficiency among listed flex workspace operators
~48,000 new desks sold in FY26 (record high); >50% from existing member expansions
86.9% portfolio occupancy (mature centers at 88.9%) — near-saturation, pricing power
₹475 Cr managed office deal with TMUS India signed in Q4 — large enterprise momentum
Wework India Management Ltd.quote: "FY26 was a defining year. We doubled PAT, turned net-debt negative for the first time, and continued expanding with pricing discipline. The compounding flywheel is increasingly visible — and WeWork India is no longer just a workspace operator."
